name: Paracetamol
description: A widely used over-the-counter medication for pain relief and fever reduction.
uses: Mild to moderate pain, headache, muscle aches, arthritis, backache, toothaches, colds, and fevers.
dosage: Adults: 500-1000mg every 4-6 hours, max 4000mg/day. Children: weight-based dosing required.
side_effects: Generally well tolerated. Rare: skin rash, liver damage with overdose.
warnings: Do not exceed recommended dose. Avoid with severe liver disease. Check other medications for acetaminophen content.
interactions: Warfarin (increased bleeding risk), alcohol (liver toxicity), isoniazid.

name: Ibuprofen
description: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug used for pain, fever, and inflammation.
uses: Pain relief, fever reduction, inflammation from arthritis, menstrual cramps, minor injuries.
dosage: Adults: 200-400mg every 4-6 hours, max 1200mg/day OTC. Prescription doses may be higher.
side_effects: Stomach upset, nausea, dizziness, headache, increased blood pressure.
warnings: Take with food. Avoid in third trimester of pregnancy, kidney disease, active GI bleeding.
interactions: Aspirin, anticoagulants, ACE inhibitors, lithium, methotrexate.

name: Amoxicillin
description: Broad-spectrum penicillin antibiotic effective against many bacterial infections.
uses: Respiratory infections, ear infections, urinary tract infections, skin infections, H. pylori.
dosage: Adults: 250-500mg every 8 hours or 500-875mg every 12 hours. Complete full course.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, rash. Allergic reactions in penicillin-sensitive patients.
warnings: Requires prescription. Complete entire course. Report allergic reactions immediately.
interactions: Methotrexate, oral contraceptives (reduced efficacy), probenecid, allopurinol.

name: Metformin
description: First-line oral medication for type 2 diabetes mellitus.
uses: Type 2 diabetes management, polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), prediabetes.
dosage: Start 500mg once or twice daily with meals. Max 2550mg/day in divided doses.
side_effects: GI upset, nausea, diarrhea, metallic taste. Rare: lactic acidosis.
warnings: Hold before contrast imaging. Monitor kidney function. Avoid excessive alcohol.
interactions: Contrast dye, alcohol, cimetidine, certain diuretics.

name: Lisinopril
description: Angiotensin-converting enzyme inhibitor for cardiovascular conditions.
uses: Hypertension, heart failure, post-myocardial infarction, diabetic nephropathy.
dosage: Hypertension: 10mg once daily, may increase to 40mg. Take at same time daily.
side_effects: Dry cough, dizziness, hyperkalemia, headache, fatigue.
warnings: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Monitor potassium and kidney function. Angioedema risk.
interactions: Potassium supplements, NSAIDs, lithium, aliskiren.

name: Atorvastatin
description: Cholesterol-lowering medication that reduces cardiovascular risk.
uses: High cholesterol, prevention of cardiovascular events, familial hypercholesterolemia.
dosage: 10-80mg once daily, preferably in evening. Dose based on LDL targets.
side_effects: Muscle pain, joint pain, headache, GI symptoms. Rare: rhabdomyolysis.
warnings: Monitor liver enzymes and CK. Report unexplained muscle pain. Avoid grapefruit juice.
interactions: Grapefruit juice, cyclosporine, gemfibrozil, certain antifungals, macrolide antibiotics.

name: Omeprazole
description: Reduces stomach acid production for acid-related disorders.
uses: GERD, peptic ulcers, H. pylori eradication (with antibiotics), Zollinger-Ellison syndrome.
dosage: 20-40mg once daily before breakfast. Duration varies by condition.
side_effects: Headache, diarrhea, abdominal pain, nausea, vitamin B12 deficiency with long use.
warnings: Long-term use may increase fracture risk and C. difficile infection risk.
interactions: Clopidogrel (reduced activation), methotrexate, digoxin, iron supplements.

name: Salbutamol
description: Short-acting bronchodilator for rapid relief of bronchospasm.
uses: Asthma, COPD, exercise-induced bronchospasm, acute wheezing.
dosage: Inhaler: 1-2 puffs every 4-6 hours as needed. Nebulizer: 2.5-5mg every 4-6 hours.
side_effects: Tremor, tachycardia, nervousness, headache, muscle cramps.
warnings: Overuse indicates poor asthma control. Seek medical help if rescue inhaler needed frequently.
interactions: Beta-blockers (reduced efficacy), diuretics, digoxin, MAO inhibitors.

name: Levothyroxine
description: Synthetic thyroid hormone replacement for hypothyroidism.
uses: Hypothyroidism, thyroid cancer suppression therapy, goiter.
dosage: Typically 25-200mcg once daily on empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast.
side_effects: Usually none at correct dose. Overdose: palpitations, weight loss, insomnia.
warnings: Take consistently at same time. Many drug and food interactions affect absorption.
interactions: Calcium, iron, antacids, coffee, soy, cholestyramine, warfarin.

name: Amlodipine
description: Long-acting calcium channel blocker for hypertension and angina.
uses: Hypertension, chronic stable angina, vasospastic angina.
dosage: 2.5-10mg once daily. Elderly or hepatic impairment: start 2.5mg.
side_effects: Peripheral edema, flushing, dizziness, fatigue, palpitations.
warnings: Gradual dose titration. Caution in severe aortic stenosis and heart failure.
interactions: Simvastatin (dose limit), CYP3A4 inhibitors, grapefruit juice.

name: Sertraline
description: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor for depression and anxiety disorders.
uses: Major depressive disorder, OCD, panic disorder, PTSD, social anxiety, PMDD.
dosage: Start 50mg daily, may increase to 200mg. Take morning or evening consistently.
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, insomnia, sexual dysfunction, dry mouth.
warnings: Black box warning for suicidality in young adults. Do not stop abruptly. Serotonin syndrome risk.
interactions: MAOIs (contraindicated), tramadol, triptans, warfarin, NSAIDs.

name: Warfarin
description: Vitamin K antagonist anticoagulant preventing blood clot formation.
uses: Atrial fibrillation, DVT/PE treatment and prevention, mechanical heart valves.
dosage: Individualized based on INR monitoring. Typical maintenance 2-10mg daily.
side_effects: Bleeding (most serious), bruising, hair loss, skin necrosis (rare).
warnings: Regular INR monitoring required. Report any unusual bleeding. Many dietary interactions.
interactions: Vitamin K foods, antibiotics, NSAIDs, acetaminophen, amiodarone, many others.

name: Insulin Glargine
description: Basal insulin analogue providing 24-hour glucose control.
uses: Type 1 and type 2 diabetes requiring basal insulin therapy.
dosage: Individualized. Typically started 10 units or 0.2 units/kg once daily at same time.
side_effects: Hypoglycemia, injection site reactions, weight gain, lipodystrophy.
warnings: Never mix with other insulins in same syringe. Monitor blood glucose regularly.
interactions: Beta-blockers (mask hypoglycemia), corticosteroids, thiazolidinediones.

name: Cetirizine
description: Non-drowsy antihistamine for allergy symptom relief.
uses: Allergic rhinitis, urticaria (hives), allergic conjunctivitis.
dosage: Adults: 10mg once daily. Children 6+: 5-10mg based on age/weight.
side_effects: Drowsiness (less than first-gen), dry mouth, fatigue, headache.
warnings: Use caution with kidney impairment. Reduce dose in elderly.
interactions: CNS depressants, alcohol. Generally fewer interactions than first-generation antihistamines.

name: Azithromycin
description: Macrolide antibiotic with broad activity and convenient dosing.
uses: Respiratory infections, skin infections, STIs, pertussis, MAC prophylaxis.
dosage: 500mg day 1, then 250mg days 2-5. Alternative: single 1g dose for some infections.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain, QT prolongation (rare).
warnings: Caution in cardiac arrhythmias. Requires prescription. Complete full course.
interactions: Warfarin, digoxin, cyclosporine, antacids (reduced absorption).

name: Prednisone
description: Synthetic glucocorticoid with potent anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive effects.
uses: Asthma exacerbations, autoimmune conditions, allergic reactions, inflammatory conditions.
dosage: Highly variable: 5-60mg daily depending on condition. Taper when discontinuing.
side_effects: Weight gain, mood changes, insomnia, hyperglycemia, osteoporosis with long use.
warnings: Do not stop abruptly after prolonged use. Increases infection risk. Monitor blood sugar.
interactions: NSAIDs (GI bleeding), warfarin, vaccines (reduced efficacy), antidiabetics.

name: Losartan
description: Blocks angiotensin II receptors to lower blood pressure and protect kidneys.
uses: Hypertension, diabetic nephropathy, heart failure, stroke prevention.
dosage: 50mg once daily, may increase to 100mg. Start 25mg in volume-depleted patients.
side_effects: Dizziness, hyperkalemia, fatigue, back pain, upper respiratory infection.
warnings: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Monitor potassium and kidney function.
interactions: Potassium supplements, NSAIDs, lithium, rifampin.

name: Pantoprazole
description: PPI that suppresses gastric acid secretion for acid-related disorders.
uses: GERD, erosive esophagitis, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, H. pylori eradication.
dosage: 40mg once daily before breakfast for 4-8 weeks. Maintenance doses may be lower.
side_effects: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain, vitamin deficiencies.
warnings: Long-term PPI use associated with increased infection and fracture risks.
interactions: Methotrexate, clopidogrel, digoxin, iron and B12 supplements.

name: Tramadol
description: Centrally acting analgesic for moderate to moderately severe pain.
uses: Moderate pain, post-operative pain, chronic pain conditions.
dosage: 50-100mg every 4-6 hours as needed. Max 400mg/day. Extended-release formulations available.
side_effects: Nausea, dizziness, constipation, drowsiness, headache.
warnings: Controlled substance. Seizure risk. Serotonin syndrome with serotonergic drugs. Respiratory depression.
interactions: SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs, carbamazepine, warfarin, digoxin.

name: Doxycycline
description: Broad-spectrum tetracycline antibiotic with anti-inflammatory properties.
uses: Respiratory infections, acne, Lyme disease, malaria prophylaxis, STIs, rosacea.
dosage: 100mg twice daily or 200mg loading then 100mg daily. Take with full glass of water.
side_effects: Photosensitivity, GI upset, esophageal irritation, yeast infections.
warnings: Avoid in pregnancy and children under 8. Take upright with water. Use sun protection.
interactions: Antacids, iron, calcium, warfarin, oral contraceptives, isotretinoin.

name: Aspirin
description: Pain reliever and blood thinner
uses: Pain, fever, heart attack prevention
dosage: 75-325mg daily
side_effects: Bleeding, stomach irritation
warnings: Avoid active bleeding
interactions: Warfarin, Ibuprofen

name: Clopidogrel
description: Prevents blood clots
uses: Heart attack, stroke prevention
dosage: 75mg once daily
side_effects: Bleeding, bruising
warnings: Monitor bleeding risk
interactions: Aspirin, Warfarin

name: Rosuvastatin
description: Lowers cholesterol
uses: High cholesterol
dosage: 5-40mg daily
side_effects: Muscle pain, headache
warnings: Monitor liver function
interactions: Warfarin

name: Simvastatin
description: Lowers cholesterol levels
uses: Hyperlipidemia
dosage: 10-40mg daily
side_effects: Muscle pain, liver issues
warnings: Avoid grapefruit juice
interactions: Warfarin

name: Atenolol
description: Reduces heart workload
uses: Hypertension, angina
dosage: 25-100mg daily
side_effects: Fatigue, dizziness
warnings: Do not stop suddenly
interactions: Amlodipine

name: Bisoprolol
description: Controls heart rate
uses: Heart failure, hypertension
dosage: 2.5-10mg daily
side_effects: Fatigue, dizziness
warnings: Monitor heart rate
interactions: Amlodipine

name: Hydrochlorothiazide
description: Removes excess fluid
uses: Hypertension, edema
dosage: 12.5-50mg daily
side_effects: Low potassium, dizziness
warnings: Monitor electrolytes
interactions: Lisinopril

name: Furosemide
description: Treats fluid retention
uses: Heart failure, edema
dosage: 20-80mg daily
side_effects: Dehydration, low potassium
warnings: Monitor fluid balance
interactions: Lisinopril

name: Spironolactone
description: Treats hypertension and edema
uses: Heart failure, hypertension
dosage: 25-100mg daily
side_effects: High potassium
warnings: Monitor potassium levels
interactions: Losartan

name: Gabapentin
description: Treats nerve pain
uses: Neuropathy, seizures
dosage: 300-3600mg daily
side_effects: Drowsiness, dizziness
warnings: Avoid alcohol
interactions: Tramadol
